name: Bank Of Baroda
description_short: Bank of Baroda USA provides correspondent banking, NRI deposit accounts, and remittance services for Indian nationals and diaspora customers in the United States.
description_long:
Bank of Baroda is a century-old Indian banking institution founded in 1908 that has expanded to operate in 90 overseas offices across 15 countries. The parent bank in India maintains a significant international presence, including dedicated US operations that serve the Indian diaspora and those with connections to India. The organization has evolved from a small regional bank in Baroda to a major financial institution with modern infrastructure, including its corporate headquarters in Mumbai.

The US division specializes in products and services tailored to NRI (Non-Resident Indian) customers and businesses with India connections. Their service offerings include correspondent banking relationships, NRI-specific deposit accounts, certificate of deposit accounts denominated in US dollars, business checking accounts, advances/loans, remittance services for money transfers to India, document attestation and verification services, internet banking, and forex exchange services. They provide transparent pricing with published rates and charges across all product categories.

Bank of Baroda USA distinguishes itself through its global banking heritage, India-focused expertise, and multi-country operational experience. The bank leverages its international network and institutional knowledge of cross-border banking to serve customers with specific needs related to India. The organization maintains established relationships with correspondent banks globally and offers secured investment options like US dollar-denominated certificates of deposit. Their brand extends to celebrity partnerships, with Sachin Ramesh Tendulkar serving as Global Brand Ambassador.

The primary limitation is that Bank of Baroda USA operates as a specialized niche bank serving primarily NRI and India-connected customers rather than offering comprehensive consumer banking services to the general US population. As a correspondent banking operation, their physical branch presence appears limited, and many services require direct contact with branch representatives for complete information. The website content focuses primarily on service descriptions rather than comprehensive product details, rates, or account opening procedures.
pros: Established century-old institution with 90+ overseas offices across 15 countries providing global banking credibility
Specialized NRI banking services designed specifically for Indian nationals and diaspora customers
Correspondent banking relationships enabling international fund transfers and cross-border transactions
US dollar-denominated certificate of deposit accounts offering fixed-rate secured investments
Comprehensive remittance services for secure money transfers to India with trusted infrastructure
Internet banking and 24/7 security center access for account monitoring and protection
Document attestation, verification, and certification services for immigration and legal purposes
cons: Limited physical branch presence in USA compared to major domestic US banks, restricting walk-in accessibility
Website provides minimal specific pricing details—customers must contact branches directly for rates and charges
Services heavily tailored to NRI customers, making them less suitable for general US consumers without India connections
Correspondent banking model means they may not offer the full product range available at the Indian parent bank
Limited information available about account opening requirements, minimum balances, or complete fee structures online
best_for: Non-Resident Indians (NRIs) seeking banking services connected to India with US presence
Indian diaspora customers needing regular remittances and money transfers to family in India
International businesses requiring correspondent banking and cross-border transaction capabilities
India-connected professionals seeking certificate of deposit investments with USD denominations
diagnosis: Bank of Baroda USA is best suited for Non-Resident Indian customers and India-connected professionals seeking banking services, remittances, and international transactions with an institution that understands cross-border banking needs. The primary caveat is that this is a specialized niche bank with limited US branch presence and services primarily oriented toward the Indian diaspora rather than general US consumers.
services: NRI Bank Accounts with specialized features for non-resident Indian customers
Correspondent Banking services for international transactions and relationships
Certificate of Deposit accounts denominated in US dollars with fixed interest rates
Business Checking Accounts with no transaction restrictions and non-interest bearing structure
Bank Advances and financial assistance for individuals and enterprises
Remittance services for secure money transfers to India
Internet Banking for online account access and management
Document Attestation, Verification, and Certification services
Forex Exchange services with indicative rates
Security Center for account protection and fraud prevention
Deposit Accounts tailored to different customer needs
Interest Rates and transparent service charges across all products
